info_10_about: The fund allocates at least 80% of its assets to equity, preference shares and fully convertible debentures. The remaining is invested in money market instruments to maintain liquidity. The fund enjoys tax benefits under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act. Investors can reduce their tax liability by up to ₹46,800. Investments in the SBI long-term fund are spread across large, small and mid-cap stocks to ensure the portfolio is well diversified. The fund has a mandatory lock-in period of 3 years. Investment Strategy The fund is actively managed by Dinesh Balchandran, who follows a bottom-up stock-picking approach along with fundamental analysis. The investment process involves identifying companies with strong business models and sound management. Each stock in the portfolio is selected after a deep evaluation of its financial health. Who Should Invest In SBI Long Term Equity Fund - Direct Growth?You should invest in the SBI long-term fund if:-You are a first-time investor-You are looking for tax benefits-You want a diversified portfolio Taxation on SBI Long Term Fund Since equity-linked saving schemes have a lock-in period of 3 years, they are not subject to short-term capital gain (STCG). Any gains from selling the SBI long-term fund after 3 years are subject to long-term capital gain or LTCG at the rate of 12.5%. Allocation & Historical Returns
info_12_how_to_invest: 1. Download the INDmoney app. 2. Search for the fund. 3. Choose SIP or lump sum. 4. Enter the investment amount. 5. Complete payment using bank mandate or UPI.
info_14_fund_vs_competition: The fund has outperformed the benchmark (Nifty 500) over 3Y, 5Y, but underperformed over 1Y. 1M: -3.9% 3M: -4.36% 6M: -0.27% 1Y: 8.77% 3Y: 22.72% 5Y: 19.07%
